title: I am allowed to stop, even if not everything is done
subtitle: An affirmation for unhooking from the belief that rest requires a finished list.
excerpt: A short affirmation for Day 7 of the SLEEP program. A single statement that challenges the conditioning that rest must be earned by completion.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short affirmation for the belief that you are not allowed to rest until the list is empty. The list will never be empty. The affirmation names what is already true: the permission to stop is not contingent on completion.
I am allowed to stop, even if not everything is done.

Say it three times before bed. Say it slowly, with the emphasis on "allowed." If the statement meets resistance (a counter-argument rising up), say it again without debating with the resistance. The point is repetition, not persuasion.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 7 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
